title: Log event `data` for `type`: state.revoke
summary: Auditable marker that revocable or grant-backed state was cryptographically or policy revoked (e.g. key grant, revocable post). Pairs with content_class revocable and dual objects (§9.1, §20).

object_id.description: State object id (e.g. `post:…`) whose material is revoked for rendering.

version.description: Monotonic state version at which revoke is applied (per §4.1.1 integer rules in serialized form).

revoke_scope.description: Optional: e.g. key_grant|object|manifest — extensible; unknown values are opaque to generic clients.

target_rules.description: `target` MAY duplicate `object_id` for indexers; if both exist they MUST be consistent for v1 interop of this file.

validation_rules: `version` MUST be a JSON integer in canonical form (§4.1.1).
Origin MUST ensure revoke ordering vs state; clients MUST follow §5 on conflict with head.
For `dual` objects, state storage and this log event together define audit trail (§9.1).

compatibility.forward: Optional `revoke_scope` and other optional keys: ignore if unknown; MUST NOT treat as full semantic revoke without local policy.
compatibility.backward: Dropping `object_id` or `version` is invalid for v1.
compatibility.version_bump: New required fields → new registry file version.
